business_logic.summary: feat(chrome-extension): cache spoken-summary audio so it never re-downloads — Self-scan of `chrome-extension-features-tts` (working tree) — 0 commit(s), 24 file(s) vs `main`.

axes.name: money
axes.label: 💰 Money
axes.subtitle: Tech-debt servicing: bugs + maintenance + AI tokens
axes.formula: Δ% = (projected_savings − cost) / (160h × $95) × 100. cost = human_debt (bug_hours[Critical 8 / Important 3 / Minor 0.5] + maintenance [1.5h/finding + 0.01h/LOC]) × $95 + LLM_iteration_cost (6 iters × ~1M tokens × size × debt-multiplier × $5/1M) + infra/db uplift. NEW-feature dev-time is NOT modeled — this axis is the cost of SERVICING what the PR ships.
axes.source: Tech-debt servicing model — bug-fix hours (defect-cost escalation, testomat/cloudqa 2025-26), maintenance (Sonar: ~5,500 dev-h/yr per 1M LOC), and LLM iteration tokens (agentic ~1M tok/iteration, blended ~$5/1M — BenchLM / Stanford Digital Economy Lab 2026). dev-rate: HiBob fully-burdened US $95/hr.

additional_sources.title: BLS — Software Developer Occupational Stats (US wages)
Sonar — Cost of technical debt (dev-hours/yr per 1M LOC)
Testomat — Real cost of software bugs (defect escalation)
Stanford Digital Economy Lab — how AI agents spend tokens

axes.name: customer
axes.label: 👥 Customer / user value
axes.subtitle: Time saved + value added per session
axes.formula: Δ% = (0.6 × min(features/3, 1) × 100 + 0.4 × min((issues+fixes)/3, 1) × 100) × dampening, where dampening = 1 − 0.15 × min(critical_findings, 3).
axes.source: derived from PrCounts (Conventional Commits + GitHub linking keywords)

axes.name: runtime
axes.label: ⚙️ Software runtime
axes.subtitle: Wire size, memory, serialization
axes.formula: Δ% = optimistic_prior − regression_penalty. optimistic_prior from `perf:` commit count / net-LOC cleanup; regression_penalty sums detected runtime-degrading findings (N+1, blocking-in-async, ORM/SQL, expensive compute) by tier (Critical 12 / Important 6 / Minor 2), capped at 60.
axes.source: static findings (drift call-graph analysis) + `perf:` commit proxy

additional_sources.title: Google SRE Book — Monitoring distributed systems

axes.name: runtime_ux
axes.label: 🎨 Software runtime UX
axes.subtitle: Dev / debugging experience time delta
axes.formula: Δ% = min(60, 5·new_test_files + 3·bug_fixes + 4·docs_commits). Confidence held Low when a Critical finding ships with zero new tests.
axes.source: Conventional Commits docs/test signals + GitHub linking keywords

additional_sources.title: Google SRE Book — Being on-call (developer UX)

value_card.bottom_line: Bottom line — mixed: Runtime UX ▲ but Money ▼. Investigate the regression(s) before merge.

code_suggestions.category_label: Optimization — Call-graph N+1
code_suggestions.why_it_matters: `main` reaches `db` 13× across its subtree (cyclomatic 28). This shape often indicates an N+1: a loop calling a DB-touching callee per item. Even if the ORM isn't in drift's per-SDK matcher list, the call-graph footprint is high.
code_suggestions.remediation_hint: Batch the inner query (preload / join fetch / IN-clause). If the loop is intentional and bounded, document it.
code_suggestions.llm_prompt_hint: Drift's static analyzer flagged a `call_graph_n_plus_one` issue in `main` (rust, drift-static-profiler/src/main.rs). Produce a one-paragraph 'Why it matters' and a unified-diff suggestion. Cite the references list.

code_suggestions.category_label: Optimization — Call-graph N+1
code_suggestions.why_it_matters: `run_analyze` reaches `db` 13× across its subtree (cyclomatic 6). This shape often indicates an N+1: a loop calling a DB-touching callee per item. Even if the ORM isn't in drift's per-SDK matcher list, the call-graph footprint is high.
code_suggestions.remediation_hint: Batch the inner query (preload / join fetch / IN-clause). If the loop is intentional and bounded, document it.
code_suggestions.llm_prompt_hint: Drift's static analyzer flagged a `call_graph_n_plus_one` issue in `run_analyze` (rust, drift-static-profiler/src/main.rs). Produce a one-paragraph 'Why it matters' and a unified-diff suggestion. Cite the references list.

code_suggestions.category_label: Framework misuse — React
code_suggestions.why_it_matters: `dangerouslySetInnerHTML` injects raw, unescaped HTML — an XSS sink when the value is user-influenced and not sanitised.
code_suggestions.remediation_hint: Render text as children (auto-escaped), or sanitise with a vetted library (DOMPurify) before setting inner HTML.

before_lines.code: The same sticky comment Drift posts on the PR — rendered locally.

code_suggestions.llm_prompt_hint: Drift's static analyzer flagged a `react_antipattern` issue in `LivePipelineRun` (typescript, drift-chrome-extension/src/app/LivePipelineRun.tsx). Produce a one-paragraph 'Why it matters' and a unified-diff suggestion. Cite the references list.

code_suggestions.category_label: Product correctness — Raw SQL concatenation
code_suggestions.why_it_matters: Possible SQL injection at drift-chrome-extension/src/app/Chat.tsx:129 — a SQL keyword appears next to string interpolation. Even if the variable is currently trusted, this pattern propagates through future code edits.
code_suggestions.remediation_hint: Use parameterized queries (`?` placeholders, prepared statements, or the ORM's bind-parameter API). Never concatenate user input into a SQL string.
code_suggestions.llm_prompt_hint: Drift's static analyzer flagged a `sql_concat_injection` issue (typescript, drift-chrome-extension/src/app/Chat.tsx). Produce a one-paragraph 'Why it matters' and a unified-diff suggestion. Cite the references list.

code_suggestions.category_label: Optimization — Dead code in changed file
code_suggestions.why_it_matters: `module` in `./drift-chrome-extension/vite.config.ts` is reachable by zero callers but is in a file this PR touched. Either wire it up to an entry point or delete it.
code_suggestions.remediation_hint: Remove the symbol, or add a call from a route handler / public API.
code_suggestions.llm_prompt_hint: Drift's static analyzer flagged a `dead_code_in_changed_file` issue in `module` (typescript, ./drift-chrome-extension/vite.config.ts). Produce a one-paragraph 'Why it matters' and a unified-diff suggestion. Cite the references list.

code_suggestions.category_label: Product correctness — Sentinel value as 'missing'
code_suggestions.why_it_matters: Sentinel comparison at drift-chrome-extension/src/app/Chat.tsx:51. Using `0`/`-1`/`""`/`null` to mean 'missing' makes valid-zero/empty cases ambiguous and tends to leak into downstream code. Idiomatic fix: `Option`/`Result`/`Maybe` (or the language's null-coalescing operator).
code_suggestions.remediation_hint: Replace the sentinel with the language's optional type and let the type system enforce presence-checking at every call site.
code_suggestions.llm_prompt_hint: Drift's static analyzer flagged a `sentinel_value` issue (typescript, drift-chrome-extension/src/app/Chat.tsx). Produce a one-paragraph 'Why it matters' and a unified-diff suggestion. Cite the references list.

pr_findings_top.message: `dangerouslySetInnerHTML` injects raw, unescaped HTML — an XSS sink when the value is user-influenced and not sanitised.
pr_findings_top.remediation: Render text as children (auto-escaped), or sanitise with a vetted library (DOMPurify) before setting inner HTML.

sources.label: loc
sources.source: Robert C. Martin, Clean Code — functions should be < ~80 LOC

sources.label: schema_libs
sources.source: Library coverage for all 8 supported languages — see schema_validation.per_language_known_libraries for the full registry with citation URLs.

nfr_edge_cases.source: identifier-keyword scan inspired by SonarQube + ScopeMaster NFR detection

composite.label: do not merge as-is
notes: Advisory — does not gate the merge. Weighted geometric mean (partial non-compensability); destructive migrations apply a hard cap.

sources.label: understandability
sources.source: Bacchelli & Bird (review challenge) + SonarQube Cognitive Complexity + Lawrie naming

notes: Control-flow term uses a complexity+nesting cognitive surrogate (capped); true cognitive complexity is a planned upgrade.

components.detail: SQALE debt ratio over new code (signed delta)

sources.label: coverage gate
sources.source: SonarQube Coverage-on-New-Code (80%) — static reachability is a proxy

notes: Coverage is a STATIC reachability proxy, not executed coverage (over/under-approximates). Supply a real patch-coverage % to lift confidence.

components.detail: I/O roots with no logging (blind spots)

notes: Higher = riskier. Advisory — does not gate the merge.

components.detail: static specialization approx (Herfindahl); not bus factor

sources.label: review size
sources.source: SmartBear/Cisco study (≤200 ideal, >400 overwhelms) + Google eng-practices

notes: Advisory — process signal, does not gate the merge.
knowledge_concentration is a STATIC specialization approximation (Herfindahl over churn), NOT CHAOSS bus factor (needs git authorship).

components.detail: diff token footprint vs usable context

sources.label: review degrades with context
sources.source: SWE-PRBench: LLM review quality degrades monotonically as reviewed context grows

gauges.label: Token footprint
gauges.description: How many tokens this PR consumes when fed to a model — a direct proxy for how hard it is to reason about automatically.

gauges.label: Context window pressure
gauges.description: Does the full diff fit in a single context window, or must it be chunked? Chunking loses semantic coherence and misses cross-file coupling.

gauges.label: Agent reviewability
gauges.description: Can an LLM give useful feedback, or is the change too large and tangled to reason about reliably?

gauges.label: Semantic density
gauges.description: Tokens per logical change — distinguishes heavy boilerplate from dense, complex business logic.

gauges.label: Explainability score
gauges.description: Can an unfamiliar engineer understand the change without asking someone? Comment density, naming clarity, control-flow simplicity.

gauges.label: Context dependency
gauges.description: How much prior knowledge is needed to review this PR? Does it touch highly-coupled core abstractions or isolated modules?

gauges.label: Decision transparency
gauges.description: Are non-obvious engineering choices explained (algorithm choice, the rationale behind a magic number)?

gauges.label: Maintenance burden
gauges.description: How much will this code need to be touched again? Coupling, hardcoded values, and TODO density.

gauges.label: Debt introduced vs. resolved
gauges.description: Net technical-debt delta from this PR — debt added relative to what it cleans up.

gauges.label: Fragility index
gauges.description: How many other components quietly break if this code changes? High fan-out coupling and downstream dependents.

gauges.label: Test coverage (changed lines)
gauges.description: Not overall coverage — specifically the test reachability of the lines changed or added in this PR.

gauges.label: Repeatability
gauges.description: Are side effects isolated and deterministic? Can you run this twice and get identical results?

gauges.label: Edge case surface
gauges.description: The volume of implicit input/state assumptions — high surface means many boundary/failure conditions are easy to overlook.

gauges.label: Rollback complexity
gauges.description: If this fails in production, how hard is the rollback? Migrations, API changes, and stateful data transforms raise it.

gauges.label: Observability
gauges.description: Does this change add logging/metrics/tracing, or introduce an operational blind spot?

gauges.label: Blast radius
gauges.description: What share of the system is exposed if this breaks? Centrality, entrypoint reach, and fan-in.

gauges.label: Knowledge concentration
gauges.description: Is the change concentrated in code only a few people own? A static specialization proxy (not a git bus factor).

gauges.label: Review fatigue risk
gauges.description: Large diffs lose reviewer attention past the first half.
